answer: On September 15, 2026, the pipeline settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 incorrectly joined settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Since normalized_tax_id can appear on multiple buyer, seller, trust, and entity roles within a single closing, this caused duplicate escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily, overstating the escrow disbursement reconciliation variance KPI for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id, which matches multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily
